ksa_text: Knowledge of data subject rights under applicable data protection regulations, including rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, objection, and rights related to automated decision-making and profiling.

dcwf.top_element_label: Interpret and apply applicable laws, statutes, and regulatory documents and integrate into policy.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Interpret and apply applicable laws, statutes, and regulatory documents and integrate into policy.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of Analytics. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the Director of Analytics? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the Director of Analytics. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of Analytics. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the Director of Analytics. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
